overall_plot: Spheria follows parallel narratives on Earth and within a revolutionary virtual ecosystem simulation. Physicist Max recruits intern Min to join the Spheria Project after a previous intern's suspicious suicide, working alongside a team that observes crystalline beings called Polyans living inside their digital creation. Inside the simulation, young Le·ma apprentices under the wise Lumen Master Sa·ma, who leads her on a perilous journey through Uplift Pass to discover the legendary Fertile Field beyond the blue crystal mountains. During their quest, Sa·ma acquires a mysterious triangular artifact that grants him power to control creatures, saving Le·ma from a deadly Zalisk beast. As their final test, Le·ma must find an impossible route back over the mountains. The Earth scientists observe these developments through VR equipment, grappling with their role as creators watching their simulated world evolve. Tensions escalate when Polyan society enters upheaval with unusual behaviors emerging alongside four threatening red bubbles near their Colony. Sa·ma discovers a golden cave containing a portal to the Qubessence, the realm of gods, but power-hungry Fa·ro forces him to reveal this secret location, resulting in Sa·ma becoming the first imprisoned Polyan. The Council increasingly defers to Fa·ro's authority, eventually deciding to create a second seven-legged god. Meanwhile, the Spheria Project gains media attention through a Gizmodo article as Max and Min's relationship deepens. Project leader Graham arrives for a critical funding review, but Min's computer mysteriously crashes before essential calculations are complete. Covert activities at a submarine pier hint at external threats to the project's integrity. The narrative reaches its climax when Min discovers that team member Dana is the true villain who murdered previous intern Olivia and orchestrated attacks on Min and Max to conceal her secret DARPA weapons deal exploiting Spheria technology. Dana traps Min and Max in a hospital incinerator, but volunteer Walter helps them escape. Simultaneously, Fa·ro establishes absolute dictatorship in Spheria after eliminating the Council. Sa·ma leads a brilliant Worker rebellion, using a beam cannon to topple a tower across the Rift, creating a bridge to freedom. Le·ma steals Fa·ro's artifact and weaponizes it to control Zalisk creatures who massacre Fa·ro's Soldiers. Dana's video confession exposes her crimes to Graham and the team, and Max saves Captain Brennan from Dana's gun. The Workers successfully cross the Rift to achieve liberation while the human scientists restore integrity to their groundbreaking project, resolving both parallel stories of tyranny, rebellion, and ultimate freedom.
themes: Corruption of power and betrayal for financial gain
Truth exposed through evidence and courage
Engineering and ingenuity as tools of freedom
The cost of dictatorship versus collective action
Moral consequences of military weaponization of research
timeline: Max presents at 25th Solvay Conference on Physics in Brussels, impressing leading particle physicists (Ch 2)
Jill commits suicide at UConn Health Center library entrance, traumatizing boyfriend Tim and splattering him with blood (Ch 4)
Six weeks after previous intern's suicide, Max interviews Min for replacement position on Spheria Project (Ch 6)
Min starts work at Spheria Project, learning about virtual ecosystem with Polyan creatures (Ch 9)
Young Sa·ma discovers Lumen Grotto with old Polyan and yellow crystal, beginning his path to becoming Lumen Master (Ch 10 - flashback)
Sa·ma leads apprentice Le·ma through Uplift Pass in blue mountains to hidden regions (Ch 3)
Sa·ma and Le·ma camp at the Rift where Sa·ma drops yellow shard that transforms into rising bubble (Ch 8)
Sa·ma discovers the legendary Fertile Field and gains mysterious triangular sensor artifact (Ch 14)
Sa·ma uses new sensor's power to command Zalisk beast, saving Le·ma from certain death (Ch 14)
Le·ma must solve final test: finding way back over mountains with no return passage (Ch 16)
Team has Friday happy hour with ethnic foods and frozen drinks, Min brings Vietnamese Goi Cuon (Ch 13)
Jean uses VR equipment to observe Polyan Colony from god-like perspective inside the Source, watches construction of new hive (Ch 17)
Dana reviews logs showing Polyans entering new social zeitgeist with increased unusual behaviors (Ch 18)
Max sees Graham's enormous airship 'Buruwa Gunya' docked at UConn Health Center (Ch 19)
Mysterious covert meeting occurs at Electric Boat submarine pier during rainstorm (Ch 21)
Fourth consecutive red bubble threatens Colony; Council decides to relocate (Ch 22)
Moses Diego publishes Gizmodo article 'Scientists Play God' about Polyan experiment (Ch 25)
Sa·ma discovers golden cave with glowing plants and portal to Qubessence (Ch 28)
Fa·ro forces Sa·ma to lead him to cave to join other gods in Qubessence (Ch 30)
Sa·ma imprisoned in Council building cell, becoming first Polyan prisoner (Ch 32)
Min's computer crashes before critical funding report for Graham's visit (Ch 33)
Council creates second seven-legged god despite division; violet bubble approaches Source (Ch 34)
Min discovers secret red-lit server room beneath Qube building with military weapons research (Ch 35)
Fa·ro eliminates the Council and declares himself sole dictator of the Colony (Ch 36)
Min accesses secret computer using Olivia's Wow! Signal password and finds Tor/weapons evidence (Ch 37)
Dana reveals herself as the villain and traps Min and Max in hospital incinerator to murder them (Ch 43)
Sa·ma's plan executed: tower falls sideways across the Rift, creating a bridge for Workers to cross (Ch 44)
Le·ma launched from Sa·ma's cannon, lands on Fa·ro and steals his artifact mid-battle (Ch 46)
Walter rescues Min and Max from incinerator chimney after hearing their SOS signal (Ch 47)
Le·ma uses stolen artifact to control Zalisk herd, which attacks and decimates Soldier forces (Ch 48)
Dana's video confession plays during presentation, exposing her crimes to Graham and team (Ch 50)
Max tackles Dana to prevent her shooting Captain Brennan; Dana arrested (Ch 50)
Team watches recordings of Workers crossing bridge and Zalisk battle; Workers achieve freedom (Ch 51)
Polyans observed in prayer-like circle formation for first time, suggesting new spiritual development (Ch 51)

characters.name: Le·ma
characters.description: Young Polyan with swirling body patterns learning from Sa·ma. Demonstrates quick thinking and problem-solving ability. Nearly killed by Zalisk but saved by Sa·ma's new abilities.
updates: Volunteers to be launched from Sa·ma's cannon; heroically steals Fa·ro's artifact mid-battle and uses it to control Zalisk, turning the tide and enabling Workers' escape

characters.name: Sa·ma
characters.description: Experienced Lumen Seeker with straight-lined body patterns. Guides Le·ma through dangerous training. Discovers the Fertile Field and gains a mysterious new sensor with three triangles that allows him to control other creatures.
updates: Discovers golden cave with portal to Qubessence; imprisoned by Council for first time in Polyan history; warns against Fa·ro's influence
Orchestrates brilliant rebellion using engineering genius; creates beam cannon weapon and bridge strategy that enables Workers to escape across the Rift to freedom

characters.name: Max
characters.description: Dark-haired, thin physicist who spoke at 25th Solvay Conference. Uses e-cigarettes, often late, socially awkward. Passionate about quantum physics and the Spheria simulation. Gets drunk with team at happy hour.
updates: Relationship with Min deepens; appears older and more tired; receives media attention from Gizmodo article
Initially appears to be villain chasing Min but is actually innocent; also targeted for murder by Dana; survives incinerator trap and tackles Dana to prevent her shooting Captain Brennan

characters.name: Min
characters.description: Vietnamese-American woman with gadget obsession, especially 'spy glasses' that record everything. Smart, tech-savvy, brings Goi Cuon to office. Works reception desk but handles complex data analysis.
updates: Becomes night owl working late; relationship with Max progresses through dates and gaming; debates mathematics vs beauty with Frankie
Discovers the secret weapons research, is hunted by Max (mistakenly), nearly murdered by Dana in incinerator, escapes and provides evidence that exposes Dana's crimes

characters.name: David
characters.caste_or_role: Chair of 25th Solvay Conference, Max's friend
characters.description: Youthful-looking physicist who befriended Max through online quantum theory forums. Helped Max get speaking invitation to prestigious physics conference.

characters.name: Timothy (Tim)
characters.description: Was present when Jill died by suicide at UConn Health Center library entrance. Works on Spheria team, makes frozen drinks at happy hour.

characters.name: Jill
characters.description: Timothy's girlfriend who died by suicide, jumping from pedestal at library entrance. Her death traumatized Tim and created intern vacancy.

characters.name: Jean
characters.description: Team member who uses VR headset and 3D controller to observe Polyan Colony. Unlike colleagues, big meals energize her. Not a video game player. Forbidden from interacting with simulation.

characters.name: Sheila
characters.description: Australian knockout who found internship posting for Min. Doesn't understand computers but supportive friend. Paints toenails, calls Min 'babe.'

characters.name: Zalisk
characters.description: Massive beast with two rows of three legs, five scooping tusks, and enormous violet core crystal. Hunts buried green energy crystals. Severely aggressive. Nearly killed Le·ma before Sa·ma controlled it.

characters.name: Moses Diego
characters.description: Reporter who writes about the Qube technology and visits Max's lab to observe the Polyan civilization

characters.name: Olivia Holland
characters.description: Summer intern who keeps a work journal; deals with a breakup and produces statistical analysis

characters.name: Ti·ni
characters.description: Young Drone who lacks proper respect for higher castes; part of audience when Sa·ma shares his cave discovery

characters.name: Po·ni
characters.description: Drone who asks Sa·ma questions about the cave; stands admiringly next to Ti·ni

characters.name: Walter
characters.description: Hospital volunteer who discovers Min and Max trapped in the incinerator by following the sound of their SOS banging

characters.name: Captain Brennan
characters.description: Security chief who investigated Olivia's death and arrests Dana; nearly shot by Dana before Max intervenes

settings.name: The Source
settings.description: Bright orb/light above Spheria that governs Polyan behavior, changes color (white=day, dims at night). Polyans exist inside it.

settings.name: Polyan Colony
settings.description: Settlement where Polyans live in hives. Has central gathering area. Drones and Builders construct igloo-like pointed structures from brown blocks.

settings.name: Uplift Pass
settings.description: Large slit/passage through blue crystal mountains, named by ancient ancestors. Used by Lumen Seekers for training. Leads to forbidden knowledge areas.

settings.name: The Rift
settings.description: Massive vertical chasm at edge of Spheria's world, looks like artificial horizon. Yellow shards dropped in transform into rising bubbles. Most prominent landmark.

settings.name: Valley of Three
settings.description: Perfect triangle formation of mountains on opposite hemisphere with no rivers inside. Used for navigation at night. Flickering dots sometimes visible - believed to be gods playing games.

settings.name: Blue Crystal Mountains
settings.description: Mountain ranges made of blue crystal that emit repulsive force, making them impassable. Divide world into maze-like sections. Vertical on one side with platforms on other.

settings.name: Red Rivers
settings.description: Glowing red rivers throughout Spheria that illuminate blue mountains at night. Must be bridged to cross.

settings.name: Lumen Grotto
settings.description: Hexagonal room with vertical sides far from Colony. Contains glowing yellow crystal. Resting place where confident explorers meet old Lumen Masters for first test.

settings.name: The Fertile Field
settings.description: Legendary field of endless energy that isn't always visible/accessible. Sa·ma discovered it and gained his new sensor there. Source of the mysterious artifact.

settings.name: UConn Health Center
settings.description: Medical-dental school built 1970 on highest point in Hartford County. Expanded in 90s for clinical research. Houses Spheria Project. Site of Jill's suicide at library entrance with Spheres sculptures.

settings.name: Hôtel de Ville de Bruxelles
settings.description: Brussels city hall tower where 25th Solvay Conference on Physics held. Balcony overlooks city where Max and David talked after Max's presentation.

settings.name: Spheria Project Office
settings.description: Makeshift office space at UConn Health Center with glass reception desk, cubicles, conference room, kitchen. Team has Friday happy hours with ethnic food and frozen drinks.

settings.name: UConn Health Center Rooftop
settings.description: Rooftop with mooring mast disguised as cell tower; where Graham's luxury airship 'Buruwa Gunya' docks

settings.name: Buruwa Gunya Airship
settings.description: Graham's enormous yellow luxury airship with open floor plan, sunken booths, kitchen, and private rear quarters

settings.name: Electric Boat Pier 3, Groton CT
settings.description: Dark submarine pier with Virginia-class submarine, yellow crane, and cargo containers; site of mysterious meeting

settings.name: Golden Cave
settings.description: Cave discovered by Sa·ma with golden glowing plants and walls; contains portal to Qubessence where gods reside

settings.name: Council Prison Cell
settings.description: Triangular cell at base of Council building, former shelter against Zalisk; now used to imprison Sa·ma

settings.name: Secret server room beneath Qube building
settings.description: Red-lit duplicate server room under the floor with submarine-style hatch, running Tor network and Gridway distributed computing for military weapons research; connected to incinerator via red pipe

settings.name: Hospital incinerator/chimney system
settings.description: Hospital basement incinerator where Dana traps Min and Max to murder them; contains corpse storage and chimney with clean-out door through which they escape

settings.name: The Great Divide bridge
settings.description: The fallen tower now spanning the Rift, allowing Workers to cross to the other side for the first time

chapters.title: Shard of Life
chapters.synopsis: Nine Polyan Soldiers hunt a massive Zalisk beast for its violet core crystal. The Source governs all behavior. They work as team, having learned from past deaths, to surround and kill the creature for its life-giving crystal.

chapters.title: 1% Inspiration, 99% Funding
chapters.synopsis: Max smokes e-cigarette on Brussels balcony after impressing particle physicists at 25th Solvay Conference. His friend David, conference chair, congratulates him. Max reflects on quantum theory heroes and his passion for understanding atomic components.

chapters.title: Uplift Pass
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma follows Sa·ma far from Colony to blue mountains. They enter Uplift Pass, a crevice through impassable mountains. Sa·ma reveals she'll gain secret knowledge few receive and someday replace him as Lumen Master with status equal to six-legged Leaders.

chapters.title: Cell Division
chapters.synopsis: Timothy and Jill approach UConn library at night after pizza dinner. Tim stops to tie shoe at Spheres sculpture. Loud smack heard, blood spatters Tim's new white sneakers. Jill has jumped to her death from the pedestal, splattering Tim with blood.

chapters.title: Arcane Physics
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma must follow Sa·ma down steep mountain by bouncing off outcroppings through repulsive blue crystal fields. After initial terror, she learns to glide and steer. They discuss that there's no return passage - finding the way back is one of her tests.

chapters.title: First Impressions
chapters.synopsis: Max waits for slow elevator, frustrated when kids press all buttons. He philosophizes about elevator design flaws. Arrives late to interview Min for intern position to replace previous intern who committed suicide six weeks prior.

chapters.title: The Interview
chapters.synopsis: Min tells roommate Sheila about amazing interview for computer science dream internship. She'll help scientists watch virtual ecosystem with creatures called Spherians living inside computer Sphere. Most intelligent ones called Polyans. Min recorded interview with spy glasses and shows Sheila footage.

chapters.title: The Rift
chapters.synopsis: Sa·ma and Le·ma camp at the Rift, massive chasm at world's edge. They observe opposite hemisphere at night with Valley of Three triangle formation. Sa·ma drops yellow shard into Rift where it slowly transforms into rising bubble, teaching Le·ma about world's mysteries.

chapters.title: First Day
chapters.synopsis: Min starts work at glass reception desk with three monitors. Max explains she'll greet rare visitors but mainly work on projects. Team meeting introduces everyone. Min begins assignment analyzing data about Polyan lifecycle and behavior patterns.

chapters.title: The Fertile Field
chapters.synopsis: Flashback: Young Sa·ma explored beyond Colony and found Lumen Grotto with glowing yellow crystal and old Polyan. The elder was expecting him - confident explorers eventually find the grotto and pass first test. Beginning of Sa·ma's journey to become Lumen Master.

chapters.title: Min's Assignment
chapters.synopsis: Min arrives at work with coffee. Max gives her memory card with files. She browses his bookshelf including worn copy of 'A Short History of Nearly Everything' by Bill Bryson, his favorite book he reads annually. Max wishes for updated edition covering quantum physics.

chapters.title: Return Journey
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma wakes alone at camp - Sa·ma left during night. She sees yellow bubble rising from Rift where shard was dropped. She begins return journey, moving slower to observe environment. Encounters red river and reflects on her rapid learning and future as Lumen Master.

chapters.title: Happy Hour
chapters.synopsis: Friday 6pm happy hour at office. Min brings Vietnamese Goi Cuon (salad rolls) with Nuoc cham sauce. Tim makes frozen drinks with blender. Team bonds over ethnic foods. Shows collaborative, social side of research team despite being scientists.

chapters.title: Extra Sense
chapters.synopsis: Zalisk about to kill Le·ma when Sa·ma's voice commands it to leave. Sa·ma has new indigo sensor with three triangles - artifact gift from gods found at Fertile Field. Gives him greater insight and ability to control other creatures. Will pass to Le·ma someday.

chapters.title: Starlight Walk
chapters.synopsis: Drunk Max and Min leave office for night walk. Max staggers through parking lot, sits in unlocked Mercedes. They walk down hill toward pond. Max lies on grass looking at stars, discusses his passion for physics and connection to universe.

chapters.title: Cliffhanger
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma and Sa·ma return toward mountains. Sa·ma reveals no return passage exists - she must create one. This is traditional final test of Lumen Walk. He hints she must use her knowledge to find/make a way over the nearly vertical mountains.

chapters.title: God from the Machine
chapters.synopsis: Jean uses VR headset and 3D SpaceMouse controller to observe Polyan Colony from inside the Source. She flies through virtual world, watches Drones and Builders construct new hive. Wants to help but forbidden from interacting - only engineers can, and only away from Polyans.

chapters.title: Prime Directive
chapters.synopsis: Dana reviews activity logs showing increased unusual Polyan behavior. The anthropology team for the first time exceeds engineering in productivity. Jean's notes indicate Polyans are moving toward a new social zeitgeist, with interesting behaviors happening during their sleep cycles.

chapters.title: Crossing Over
chapters.synopsis: Max drives to work late and sees Graham's enormous yellow airship 'Buruwa Gunya' moored atop the UConn Health Center. He experiences the Ponzo Illusion making the airship appear larger than it is. He finds the stairway to the roof to meet Graham.

chapters.title: Running Out
chapters.synopsis: Dana and Max ascend to the rooftop and board Graham's luxury airship. They are impressed by its enormous size and elegant interior with sunken booths, kitchen, and suspended tables. They prepare to meet with Graham about the project's progress and funding.

chapters.title: Cat and Mouse
chapters.synopsis: A mysterious driver in a black sedan arrives at Electric Boat pier in Groton during a rainstorm. Near a Virginia-class submarine and cargo containers under a yellow crane, a covert exchange or meeting takes place in the darkness.

chapters.title: In Our Image
chapters.synopsis: Sa·ma reports the fourth consecutive red bubble to the Council of eight Leaders. They debate moving the Colony as the red river threatens dwellings. Sa·ma cannot explain why the gods are angry, concealing his encounters with Pi·ro and Ju·ro. The Council decides they must relocate.

chapters.title: Nourishment
chapters.synopsis: Max and Min have dinner and discuss religion. Min was raised Catholic but doesn't dwell on it. Max believes the universe is mathematical and algorithmic, suggesting God created fundamental rules and everything self-assembled from there. Their philosophical discussion deepens their connection.

chapters.title: Pro Creation
chapters.synopsis: Min visits Max's minimalist apartment with impressive gaming setup and book-filled bedroom. They play Minecraft together while drinking Blue Moon beer with orange slices. The evening demonstrates their compatibility and growing relationship beyond work.

chapters.title: Start the Press
chapters.synopsis: Moses Diego from Gizmodo publishes an article titled 'Scientists Play God In This Virtual Experiment' about visiting Max's lab. He reports on the Polyans' sentience, caste system, and creation of a seven-legged god. The article brings public attention to the project.

chapters.title: Conspiracy
chapters.synopsis: Fa·ro surveys the Colony from the central dais, resentful that Leaders treat him as a servant rather than with divine reverence. He notices a Drone watching him and considers having Sa·ma build a statue of him. His ego and desire for worship grow stronger.

chapters.title: The World Builder
chapters.synopsis: Min analyzes numerical data when Frankie startles her. They debate whether the world is mathematical formulas or beautiful randomness. Frankie invites Min out to collect textures for Spheria, showing her the real-world patterns that inspire the virtual environment.

chapters.title: Streets of Gold
chapters.synopsis: Sa·ma returns dazed to the Colony and tells Drones (including Ti·ni and Po·ni) about discovering a cave with golden glowing plants and walls. He describes sensing something below ground and finding the cave entrance, though he doesn't reveal encountering gods inside.

chapters.title: The Deleted File
chapters.synopsis: Olivia Holland's intern journal entries reveal her first weeks: dealing with boyfriend Randy's email breakup, getting drunk with roommate Ashley, and struggling with hangovers at work. She worries Max will discover her lack of productivity but has no deadlines yet.

chapters.title: God Like You
chapters.synopsis: Fa·ro forces Sa·ma, Ga·zo, and Za·zo to lead him to the golden cave and portal to the Qubessence. Fa·ro insists it's his destiny to join the other gods. Sa·ma worries because the cave god told him to return only when ready, not to bring others.

chapters.title: The Exchange
chapters.synopsis: Ashley is home alone during a thunderstorm watching The Walking Dead. She hears knocking that doesn't match the TV show. An unexplained supernatural or mysterious event begins to unfold in the dark apartment.

chapters.title: Confinement
chapters.synopsis: Sa·ma is imprisoned in the triangular Council building cell, the Colony's first prison. Ga·zo visits to confirm he's alive. They argue about Fa·ro's influence: Ga·zo praises the god's decisive leadership, while Sa·ma warns that nothing is unstoppable and closed minds will doom the Colony.

chapters.title: Coffee Break
chapters.synopsis: Min works late before Graham's important visit. Alone in the office, she makes coffee but returns to find her computer crashed with a blue screen system failure. The malfunction threatens the critical calculations needed for tomorrow's funding report.

chapters.title: Putsch
chapters.synopsis: The Council creates a second seven-legged god despite division. Fa·ro's agreement to share attention seems selfless, increasing Leaders' respect. They prepare the ritual with seven-sided core crystal and orange leg crystals as a violet bubble approaches the Source, triggering replication urges.

chapters.title: The Shadow Room
chapters.synopsis: Min discovers a secret server room beneath the Qube building floor, accessed through a submarine-style hatch. Red cables and a red pipe from the incinerator descend through it. She finds evidence of data theft and military weapons development using Tor networks.

chapters.title: Enslaved
chapters.synopsis: Fa·ro has eliminated the Council and declares himself sole ruler of the Colony. He drags Sa·ma before him and announces the Leaders are dead. Fa·ro addresses all Polyans, blaming the Council's death on their failed god-creation attempt and declaring a new era under his dictatorship.

chapters.title: Split Decision
chapters.synopsis: Min accesses the secret computer using Olivia's password (6EQUJ5 from the Wow! Signal). She discovers Gridway distributed computing software and Tor network connections, realizing the system is being used for secret military weapons research on the dark web.

chapters.title: The Plan
chapters.synopsis: Hi·ma and Pu·ma travel off-trail to a secret meeting called by Sa·ma. Twelve lead Workers arrive in darkness to avoid Soldier detection. Sa·ma has arranged this meeting before the tower incident to discuss something urgent about Fa·ro's tower project.

chapters.title: Mala Sanctis
chapters.synopsis: Max discovers Min in the secret room and chases her. She kicks a door into his face and flees, but he trips her. During the struggle, a ladder falls and knocks hundreds of Qubes loose. Min escapes up an elevator shaft while Max is left behind with the damaged equipment.

chapters.title: The Machine
chapters.synopsis: Ga·zo reports to Fa·ro that all Workers have vanished overnight. Using his artifact, Fa·ro sees them gathered beyond the tower in the distance. He realizes they've organized a mass rebellion and orders the Soldiers to pursue and eliminate them.

chapters.title: Button Mashup
chapters.synopsis: Min bursts into Dana's office claiming Max is building a super weapon and murdered Olivia. Dana locks the door and listens to Min's story about the secret server room. Dana appears sympathetic but then traps Min in the Experience Room with Max, who has just arrived.

chapters.title: Storm Wind
chapters.synopsis: Ga·zo leads Soldiers through the forest to pursue the Workers. They encounter a mysterious blue rock wall perpendicular to their path, blocking their advance. The wall stretches endlessly in both directions. Ga·zo orders Soldiers to find its edges while he waits.

chapters.title: The Incinerator
chapters.synopsis: Dana confronts Min and Max together. Both claim the other is responsible for the secret server room and weapons development. Dana reveals she's the true culprit, working with DARPA for funding and personal gain. She activates the incinerator to murder them both.

chapters.title: The Great Divide
chapters.synopsis: Sa·ma's plan unfolds: Workers pull a rope releasing a beam that destroys two tower support columns. The tower falls sideways, spanning the Rift to create a bridge to the other side. Hi·ma and others begin crossing single-file on the fallen structure.

chapters.title: Ashes to Ashes
chapters.synopsis: Min and Max are trapped in the incinerator chamber as it heats up and oxygen depletes. They find a corpse on a gurney and use it as a ladder to reach the exhaust vent. Min climbs into the chimney system searching for an escape route.

chapters.title: Into Battle
chapters.synopsis: The Soldiers attack Workers at the bridge, killing many. Le·ma asks Sa·ma to load her into his cannon weapon. He launches her through the air toward Fa·ro. She lands on him, steals his artifact, and freezes—the artifact is controlling the Zalisk!

chapters.title: Clean-out
chapters.synopsis: Hospital volunteer Walter hears banging in a pattern (SOS in Morse code) coming from the incinerator chimney. He unlocks the clean-out door and discovers Min inside. She and Max are rescued from the incinerator just in time.

chapters.title: Liberation
chapters.synopsis: A herd of Zalisk bursts onto the battlefield, attacking only Soldiers and ignoring Workers. Le·ma has used Fa·ro's stolen artifact to control them. Workers escape across the bridge while Zalisk decimate the Soldier ranks. Fa·ro battles a Zalisk that pins him down.

chapters.title: Projection
chapters.synopsis: Dana arrives disheveled to present the quarterly report to Graham and the team. Max and Min are absent. Dana begins her presentation, but something seems off. Graham notices Max is missing and questions Dana about his whereabouts.

chapters.title: Interruptions
chapters.synopsis: During Dana's presentation, a video plays showing Dana's confession to murdering Olivia and attempting to kill Max and Min for discovering her DARPA weapons deal. Captain Brennan arrives to arrest her. Dana pulls a gun but Max tackles her, saving Brennan's life.

chapters.title: Hand of God
chapters.synopsis: The team watches Spheria recordings showing the tower falling to create a bridge, then the battle where Zalisk attacked Soldiers. They see a circle of Polyans in a prayer-like formation. The chapter ends with the revelation that the Workers successfully crossed to freedom on the other side of the Rift.

objects.name: Core Crystals
objects.description: Violet crystals that pulse with light and give all Spherian beings life. Located at center of creatures' bodies. Without them, beings are mere inanimate connected tetrahedrons.
objects.significance: Fundamental life force of all Spherian creatures; primary target of Polyan hunts

objects.name: Green Crystals
objects.description: Small crystals containing seed energy, buried underground. Zalisk beasts dig them up for food.
objects.significance: Energy source for herbivorous creatures in Spheria's ecosystem

objects.name: Yellow Shards/Crystals
objects.description: Small yellow crystals that transform into rising bubbles when dropped into the Rift. Found in Lumen Grotto and Fertile Field.
objects.significance: Teaching tool and mysterious energy source; connected to Lumen Seeker knowledge

objects.name: Blue Crystal Mountains
objects.description: Mountains that emit repulsive force preventing contact. Divide world into maze-like unreachable sections.
objects.significance: Physical barriers structuring Spherian geography; challenge for Le·ma's final test

objects.name: Triangular Sensor Artifact
objects.description: Indigo sensor with three triangles pointing different directions. Appears on Sa·ma's core after visiting Fertile Field.
objects.significance: Gift from gods granting ability to control other creatures and greater world insight

objects.name: Spy Glasses
objects.description: Thick-rimmed glasses with built-in camera from spygear.com. Min's favorite gadget that records everything.
objects.significance: Min recorded entire job interview; represents human surveillance of Spheria project

objects.name: VR Headset and 3D SpaceMouse
objects.description: Virtual reality equipment allowing researchers to navigate and observe inside Spheria simulation. Controller has programmable buttons.
objects.significance: Human interface for god-like observation of Polyan world; Jean uses it to fly through Source

objects.name: Spheres Sculptures
objects.description: Bronze sculptures by Wopo Holop at UConn library entrance showing retina, dividing frog eggs, Earth, acorn, DNA strand, faceted and smooth spheres.
objects.significance: Site of Jill's suicide; thematic connection between cell division, spheres, and Spheria simulation

objects.name: Seven-sided Core Crystal
objects.description: Empty crystal used in ritual to create seven-legged Polyans; placed at center of Council Chamber
objects.significance: Essential component for creating gods; used to birth second seven-legged Polyan

objects.name: Orange Elongated Crystals
objects.description: Seven crystals placed near each facet of the core during god creation ritual
objects.significance: Part of the god-creation process; positioned by Leaders during ceremony

objects.name: Golden Cave Plants
objects.description: Glowing plants in the cave that emit light beams when broken; can heal themselves
objects.significance: Mark the path to the portal to the Qubessence; part of the mystical cave system

objects.name: Blue Rock Wall
objects.description: Wall built along the red river to protect Colony hives from destruction
objects.significance: One of Sa·ma's insightful designs; demonstrates Polyan engineering advancement

objects.name: Sa·ma's beam cannon
objects.description: Blue rock device that fires projectiles or Polyans by pulling a rope mechanism; used to topple the tower and launch Le·ma at Fa·ro
objects.significance: Key weapon that enabled the Workers' rebellion by destroying tower supports and launching Le·ma to steal Fa·ro's artifact

objects.name: Fa·ro's artifact
objects.description: Device that allows detection of living creatures through obstacles (violet outlines) and control of Zalisk herds; stolen by Le·ma
objects.significance: Critical tool that gave Fa·ro power but enabled Workers' victory when Le·ma used it to turn Zalisk against Soldiers

objects.name: Blue rock wall barrier
objects.description: Continuous vertical wall of blue rock erected perpendicular to Soldiers' path, blocking their pursuit of Workers
objects.significance: Defensive fortification built by Workers to slow Soldier advance during their escape across the bridge

objects.name: Video confession evidence
objects.description: Recording of Dana admitting to murdering Olivia and attempting to murder Min and Max for DARPA weapons deal
objects.significance: Incriminating evidence that exposes Dana's crimes and saves the Spheria Project from military corruption

factions.name: Polyan Castes
factions.description: Hierarchical society with specialized roles: 3-legged Drones (laborers), 4-legged Builders (construction), 5-legged Soldiers (hunters/warriors), 6-legged Leaders (rulers), and 5-legged Lumen Seekers (knowledge keepers with special status).

factions.name: Lumen Seekers
factions.description: Elite knowledge keepers who explore far from Colony, understand world's mysteries, and pass down secret knowledge. Status equal to or higher than six-legged Leaders. Pass artifact sensor between generations.
members: unnamed old Polyan in Lumen Grotto

factions.name: Spheria Project Team
factions.description: Scientists at UConn Health Center running virtual ecosystem simulation. Observe but forbidden to interact with Polyans. Mix of physicists, programmers, and researchers. Friday happy hours with ethnic food.

factions.name: Fa·ro's Followers
factions.description: Polyans who support Fa·ro's growing authority and decisive leadership over the traditional Council gridlock

factions.name: DARPA conspirators
factions.description: Military research agency that secretly funded weapons research through Dana, circumventing Graham's anti-military contract

factions.name: Liberated Workers
factions.description: Workers who successfully rebelled against Fa·ro's dictatorship and crossed the Rift to freedom using the fallen tower as a bridge

physics.blue_crystal: Blue crystal mountains emit a repulsive force that pushes everything away. Polyans cannot touch blue surfaces directly. This repulsion allows floating/gliding above blue surfaces and is used to climb mountains via pendulum motion on the Uplift Steps platforms.
physics.red_crystal: Red rock rivers ATTRACT everything. Living things (with violet cores) stick to red surfaces but can detach with effort. Non-living things turn red permanently and sink into red rivers. Red rivers widen and spread when red bubbles reach the Source.
physics.brown_rock: Brown rock is neutral terrain - the standard ground surface with no special attractive or repulsive properties. Green plants and structures can be built on brown rock.
physics.green_plants: Green polyhedron-shaped plants grow on brown rock terrain. Some form arched shapes large enough to walk under. Plants have no violet core, so they turn red when touching red rivers. They contain seed energy buried in the ground - a lower-quality food source foraged by Drones.
physics.violet_core: The violet crystal core is the life force of all living beings in Spheria. It stores life energy and pulses with light. When the core runs empty, the being dies and their physical structure falls apart.
physics.source_gravity: Gravity pulls outward toward the sphere's interior surface where Polyans live. The Source itself pushes outward (repels) - objects cannot reach it directly; the higher you go, the stronger the outward push.
physics.rift_description: The Rift is an uncrossable chasm dividing the world into two hemispheres. It appears as a vertical artificial horizon. Bubbles rise slowly from the Rift carrying different colors that change the Source's behavior. Below the Rift lies Qubessence, the afterlife realm.

source_colors.effect: Forage/hunt - compels all creatures to seek energy through normal hunting and foraging behavior
Growth - vegetation grows new branches and spreads
source_colors.triggered_by: Green bubble rising from Rift and hitting Source

source_colors.effect: Breeding/reproduction - creatures capable of reproduction begin to procreate
source_colors.triggered_by: Violet bubble rising from Rift and hitting Source

source_colors.effect: Mountains grow and spread - natural disaster equivalent, blue tetrahedrons sprout on open faces
source_colors.triggered_by: Blue bubble rising from Rift and hitting Source

source_colors.effect: Rivers widen and spread - natural disaster equivalent, red rock expands
source_colors.triggered_by: Red bubble rising from Rift and hitting Source

source_colors.effect: Rest/abundance - all life crystals fill with energy from within, no hunting needed for that day
source_colors.triggered_by: Yellow bubble rising from Rift (triggered by Lumen Seeker throwing yellow shard into Rift during offering ritual)

world.world_shape: Hollow sphere - Polyans live on the inside surface with the Source floating at the center. Gravity pulls outward toward the interior surface.
world.creation_story: Ra·ju (8-legged creator god, white core) created the world. His 8 children each detached as one of his legs and changed color to become individual gods: Ta·ro (brown) created land/earth; Ke·ro (orange) created life and creatures; Pi·ro (red) created rivers and red rock, decreed everything touching red turns red; Ju·ro (blue) created mountains and divisions; Ca·ro (green, female) created plants and vegetation; Na·ro (yellow) created light and the Source's glow; Wi·ro (violet) created multiplication and reproduction; Su·ro (indigo) created senses. A legend tells of the Troaten, a massive tentacled creature that spun defensively and became the Source/Rift.

caste_system.role: Menial labor - pushing blocks, making rhythmic sounds at celebrations, foraging for buried green crystals
caste_system.behaviors: Eat last and sometimes get nothing. Perform manual labor. Generally too insecure to venture far from Colony. Examples: Ti·ni, Po·ni

caste_system.role: Construction, building hives, skilled labor, engineering projects
caste_system.behaviors: More skilled than Drones. Build Colony structures and complex projects. Examples: Pu·ma, Hi·ma. Can organize and lead complex construction like the tower.

caste_system.role: Hunting, combat, protection of Colony, enforcement
caste_system.behaviors: Hunt Zalisk in coordinated teams. Protect Colony. Led by Ga·zo and Za·zo. Use whips made of connected structures as weapons. Aggressive and loyal to leadership.

caste_system.social_rank: Highest natural caste, eat first, 'just one leg short of a god'
caste_system.behaviors: Exactly 8 Leaders who govern by majority vote (tie = no action): Wu·sa, Yo·sa, Me·sa, Ju·sa, Ki·sa, Co·sa, Pi·sa, Vu·sa. Meet in Council Chamber atop Council structure. Were eliminated by Fa·ro in a coup.

Gods appear as 7-legged Polyans with colored cores. No Polyan naturally born with 7 legs until Leaders engineered Fa·ro. Fa·ro seized absolute power, eliminated the Council, and became sole ruler. Le·ma apparently created as second 7-legged being.

energy.how_they_feed: Polyans feed by touching their violet core's lower point to a violet shard and draining energy into themselves. Primary food source is energy harvested from Zalisk (drain the beast's violet core using clear crystal shards as conduits, then consume stored violet energy). Secondary food source is green crystals buried in ground containing seed energy.
energy.crystal_colors: Violet crystals contain life energy. Green crystals contain seed energy (lower quality). Clear crystals serve as conduits for draining energy. Yellow crystals found in Proving Ground used for Lumen offering ritual.
energy.hierarchy_of_feeding: Leaders eat first, Soldiers second, Workers third, Drones last (sometimes get nothing). After Fa·ro's coup, he controls all feeding.
energy.death_state: When a Polyan's violet core runs empty, they die. Their physical structure (polygon shell) falls apart. Their 'structure' descends into the Rift to Qubessence, the afterlife.

sensors.description: Polyans have indigo sensors on their cores. Each sensor has a specific function.
sensors.types: Eye/vision sensor, ear/hearing sensor, mouth/speech sensor. Sensors can be vacant 'ports' awaiting attachment.
sensors.artifact_sensor: Sa·ma possesses a three-sided indigo rod with tetrahedrons on each end, given by god Pi·ro. It attaches to a vacant sensor port and grants ability to see beyond normal perception and control/command creatures (used to control Zalisk herd). Can sense living creatures outlined in violet even through obstructions, and can tune to track specific objects (like the tower, seen in yellow).

polyans.biology: Polyans are crystalline beings consisting of connected tetrahedrons forming a roundish body with evenly-spaced legs around their violet core. They look like multi-legged spiders made of geometric shapes. Size approximately equal to a single Zalisk leg. Female Polyans have swirling patterns; males have straight textured lines.
polyans.gender: Polyans have gender distinctions. Females have swirling body patterns (examples: Le·ma, Ca·ro the goddess). Males have straight textured lines (example: Sa·ma). The goddess Ca·ro is explicitly female.
polyans.naming_convention: Names follow pattern of syllables separated by middle dot: Sa·ma, Le·ma, Fa·ro, Ga·zo, Za·zo, Ti·ni, Po·ni, Pu·ma, Hi·ma, Do·ma. Leader names end in ·sa: Wu·sa, Yo·sa, Me·sa, Ju·sa, Ki·sa, Co·sa, Pi·sa, Vu·sa. God names end in ·ro: Ra·ju, Ta·ro, Ke·ro, Pi·ro, Ju·ro, Ca·ro, Na·ro, Wi·ro, Su·ro.

gods.name: Ra·ju
gods.role_in_creation: Created the world and had 8 legs that became his children, the other gods. Has 8 legs.

gods.name: Ta·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Detached as Ra·ju's leg, created the brown rock terrain and land formations

gods.name: Ke·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Detached as Ra·ju's leg, created living creatures and life force

gods.name: Pi·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Detached as Ra·ju's leg, created red rivers and red rock. Hot-tempered, decreed everything touching red turns red. Gave Sa·ma the artifact sensor.

gods.name: Ju·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Detached as Ra·ju's leg, created blue mountains that divide the world into sections

gods.name: Ca·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Detached as Ra·ju's leg (female goddess), created green plants and vegetation

gods.name: Na·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Detached as Ra·ju's leg, created the light of the Source and illumination

gods.name: Wi·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Detached as Ra·ju's leg, created the ability to multiply and reproduce

gods.name: Su·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Detached as Ra·ju's leg, created the sensors (eyes, ears, mouth) that allow perception

afterlife.name: Qubessence
afterlife.description: Realm below the Rift with violet landscape, violet mountains, violet sky. Contains endless energy with no need to hunt.
afterlife.how_to_reach: Only 'structures' (dead Polyans whose cores have emptied) can enter. Their structures descend into the Rift after death.
afterlife.warning: Living Polyans who jump into the Rift are destroyed agonizingly - Qubessence only accepts structures/dead bodies, not living beings with active cores. This secret is kept by Lumen Seekers and Masters.

lumen_tradition.lumen_master: Colony's spiritual and knowledge leader, publicly known role. Holds special status equal to or higher than six-legged Leaders. Current Master is Sa·ma. Masters possess deep knowledge of Spheria's workings and history.
lumen_tradition.lumen_seeker: Apprentice in training to eventually replace the Master. Current Seeker is Le·ma. Learns secret knowledge passed down through generations.
lumen_tradition.lumen_walk: Rite of passage - apprentice travels with Master across dangerous Proving Ground to the Rift, performs offering ritual (throwing yellow shard into Rift to trigger yellow abundance day), then must find their own way back alone. Tests resourcefulness and courage.
lumen_tradition.lumen_elder: Retired Master who goes into seclusion in the Lumen Grotto, trains new Seeker when current Master is too busy. Do·ma was previous elder who trained Sa·ma.
lumen_tradition.lumen_grotto: Hidden hexagonal dwelling with glowing yellow crystal at center. Secret resting place far from Colony where Lumen Elder lives in seclusion. Only the excessively confident venture far enough to find it - this becomes first test for potential Seekers.

legends.name: The Fertile Field
legends.description: Legendary circle of 20 large violet crystals that replenish themselves with endless energy. Sa·ma discovered it actually exists in a hidden cave system within the mountains. The cave contains glowing plants that emit light beams when broken, illuminating passages. Deep within is a chamber with violet crystals and a portal to Qubessence, guarded by a cave god (likely Ke·ro, the orange god of life).

legends.name: The Troaten
legends.description: Mythological massive creature with many tentacles that camouflaged itself, spun defensively, and pierced cores of prey at its center. Legend says it became the Source and the Rift.

legends.name: Valley of Three
legends.description: Large perfect triangle formation of blue mountains on opposite hemisphere with no rivers inside. Used by Soldiers for navigation at night. Legend says gods go there to play games - sometimes seen as flickering dots moving about on certain nights.

rituals.name: Lumen Offering Ritual
rituals.description: Part of Lumen Walk rite of passage. Lumen Seeker throws yellow shard into the Rift, which triggers a yellow bubble to rise and eventually reach the Source, causing a day of abundance when all life crystals fill from within and no hunting is needed.

rituals.name: Celebratory Gatherings
rituals.description: Colony gathers at central staging area. Drones make rhythmic sounds. Community celebration events held in Colony center.

rituals.name: Council Voting
rituals.description: Eight Leaders meet in columned Council Chamber atop Council structure. Guards stationed at three faces prevent approach during sessions. Leaders govern by majority vote; tie votes result in no action (later Fa·ro breaks ties, then seizes absolute power).

rituals.name: God Creation Ritual
rituals.description: Complex engineering process to create 7-legged Polyan. Empty seven-sided core crystal placed in center with seven orange elongated crystals near each facet. Seven Leaders take positions and channel energy when Source turns violet (breeding color). Successfully created Fa·ro and attempted second god creation.

fauna.name: Zalisk
fauna.description: Enormous six-legged beast with violet core and shelf of five scooping tusks. Two rows of three massive legs. Single Polyan is about equal in size to one Zalisk leg.
fauna.behavior: Forages for buried green crystals. Known for severe aggression. Camouflages in vegetation. Extremely dangerous - many Polyans died before learning to hunt them in coordinated teams.
fauna.danger_level: Extreme - requires team of 9+ Soldiers with coordination to hunt safely
fauna.energy_role: Primary food source for Polyans. Soldiers drain Zalisk's violet core using clear crystal shards as conduits, harvest the life energy for Colony consumption.

fauna.name: Troaten
fauna.description: Mythological massive creature with many tentacles. Camouflaged appearance.
fauna.behavior: Spins defensively. Pierces cores of prey at its center.
fauna.energy_role: Mythological - said to have become the Source and Rift

flora.name: Green Polyhedron Plants
flora.description: Standard vegetation growing on brown rock terrain. Polyhedron-shaped. Some form large arched shapes big enough to walk under.
flora.properties: Have no violet core. Turn red when touching red rivers (consumed by red rock).
flora.uses: Part of landscape. Green crystals buried beneath ground provide seed energy food source for Drones.

flora.name: Cave Glow Plants
flora.description: Strange glowing plants found inside mountain cave system leading to Fertile Field. Form twisting maze of passages.
flora.properties: Emit light beams when broken that illuminate cave walls. Can heal/regrow after being broken.
flora.uses: Natural lighting in cave system. Breaking them creates light beams used for navigation and revealing hidden passages/portals.

crystals.found_in: Mountains throughout Spheria. Blue tetrahedrons can sprout on open faces when blue bubble reaches Source.
crystals.properties: Emits powerful repulsive force. Cannot be touched directly. Push strengthens with proximity. Allows gliding/floating above surface.
crystals.significance: Creates impassable mountain barriers dividing world into sections. Used for Uplift Steps platforms that allow crossing mountains via pendulum motion. Can be weaponized in structures/cannons using repulsive force.

crystals.found_in: Rivers throughout Spheria. Red rivers spread when red bubbles reach Source.
crystals.properties: Emits attractive force. Living things (violet cores) stick but can detach with effort. Non-living things turn red permanently and sink.
crystals.significance: Creates dangerous rivers that can consume structures. Requires careful bridge construction or walls to protect Colony. Everything touching red becomes red (Pi·ro's decree).

crystals.found_in: Standard ground terrain, loose blocks, building materials
crystals.properties: Neutral - no special attractive or repulsive properties. Stable for construction.
crystals.significance: Primary building material for hives and structures. Neutral ground for Colony establishment.

crystals.found_in: Buried in brown rock ground beneath vegetation
crystals.properties: Contains seed energy - lower quality than violet crystal energy
crystals.significance: Food source for Drones who forage by digging. Allows survival when Zalisk hunting is unsuccessful.

crystals.found_in: Cores of all living creatures. Large violet crystals in Fertile Field cave. Qubessence afterlife realm entirely violet.
crystals.properties: Stores and pulses with life energy. Core of life force for all beings. Can be drained and energy transferred.
crystals.significance: Essential for life. When empty, being dies. Primary valuable resource. Fertile Field contains 20 large self-replenishing violet crystals. Represents life force and reproduction (Wi·ro's domain).

crystals.found_in: Lumen Grotto (large glowing crystal). Proving Ground (small shards).
crystals.properties: Glows with light. Small shards can be thrown into Rift to trigger yellow abundance bubbles.
crystals.significance: Sacred to Lumen tradition. Center of Lumen Grotto where Elder dwells. Used in offering ritual during Lumen Walk. Yellow Source state creates day of abundance. Represents light and Source's glow (Na·ro's domain).

crystals.properties: Seven elongated orange crystals placed near facets of seven-sided core during creation of 7-legged Polyans
crystals.significance: Essential component in engineering gods. Represents life/creation domain of Ke·ro.

crystals.found_in: Sensors on Polyan cores. Sa·ma's artifact.
crystals.properties: Forms sensors for perception (eyes, ears, mouth). Sa·ma's artifact is three-sided indigo rod with tetrahedrons on ends.
crystals.significance: Enables senses and perception. Artifact grants enhanced vision and creature control abilities. Represents senses domain of Su·ro.

crystals.properties: Serves as conduit for transferring violet energy from Zalisk cores to storage
crystals.significance: Essential tool for harvesting energy during hunts. Allows safe energy transfer.
